common.notFoundDescription: This page doesn't exist. Maybe it never did.

hero.tagline: I build software the Portuguese government runs on.
hero.classTipHint: ↑ Hover the elements to see the Tailwind classes

about.title: About me
about.subtitle: INTRO
about.body: Four years building services for the Portuguese government, used by millions. I work in Next.js, TypeScript, .NET and React Native. I move between code and design, and lead the team technically when there's no one else around to do it.

chave-movel-digital.description: Backend on Portugal's national digital authentication service used by 8M+ citizens.
chave-movel-digital.overview: Backend on Portugal's national authentication service. <emphasis>8M+ citizens</emphasis> sign in through it to reach every public service in the country.

codebaseAge.label: CODEBASE AGE
codebaseAge.description: Legacy .NET Framework 4 system extended without rewrite.

regressions.label: REGRESSIONS
regressions.description: Across 3+ years of feature work and migrations.

integrations.label: INTEGRATIONS
integrations.description: REST + SOAP services, SSO and gov data layers.

caseStudy.problem: Keep extending a ten-year-old .NET Framework 4 system that 8M+ citizens depend on, without breaking anything, while shipping new auth flows.
caseStudy.solution: Worked the full backend. REST/SOAP integrations, auth flows, SQL Server and MySQL queries. Security and performance were non-negotiable on every call.
caseStudy.impact: Zero regressions in three years on a system 8M+ citizens use daily.

sam.description: Frontend lead for Portugal's new government address change service, replacing a legacy system.
sam.overview: Lead frontend on the new <emphasis>address change service</emphasis> for the Portuguese government. The system every citizen has to use when they move house.

team.label: TEAM
team.description: Devs and QA, coordinated when lead absent.

envs.label: ENVIRONMENTS
envs.description: Dev, staging and production via Kubernetes.

caseStudy.problem: Replace an aging system with a modern, accessible address change service. The backend wasn't ready during early development, so the frontend had to ship independently.
caseStudy.solution: Built the frontend in Next.js 15, TypeScript and Tailwind. Integrated Keycloak SSO. Mocked the backend with Microcks while it was being built. Plugged in the ARTE Design System through Storybook. Got to 86% test coverage with Jest + SonarQube. Set up the Kubernetes config and CI/CD pipelines.
caseStudy.impact: 86% automated test coverage. Stepped in to coordinate the team (4 devs + 2 QA) whenever the project lead wasn't around.

mavie-decor.description: Landing for a Portuguese event decoration business. Custom CMS so the owner manages the gallery, packs, reviews and leads herself.
mavie-decor.overview: Landing for a Portuguese event decoration business with a <emphasis>custom CMS</emphasis>. Admin panel, image uploads to Vercel Blob, leads going to WhatsApp with a DB backup.

tables.label: DB TABLES
tables.description: Contacts, gallery, before/after, packs and reviews.

admin.label: ADMIN
admin.description: Full CMS panel with auth and Vercel Blob uploads.

channel.label: LEAD CHANNEL
channel.description: Direct WhatsApp redirect with DB backup of every submission.

caseStudy.problem: Build a landing that turns visitors into WhatsApp messages. The owner needs to update gallery, before/after pairs, packs and reviews on her own. No code, no monthly CMS fee.
caseStudy.solution: Next.js 16 landing on top of a custom admin. Auth, Vercel Blob for images, Drizzle and Neon for everything else. Five tables: contacts, gallery, before/after, packs, reviews. The public side has the usual sections plus a contact form that opens WhatsApp prefilled. GSAP and motion for animations, React Compiler in the build.
caseStudy.impact: Live at maviedecor.pt. The owner runs the site herself. Every lead reaches WhatsApp and gets stored in the DB.

stay-safe.description: Mobile safety app to identify dangerous routes via community-flagged map pins.
stay-safe.overview: Mobile app to flag dangerous spots on a map, using <emphasis>community pins</emphasis> users could vote on. Final-year academic project. The school still uses the report.

grade.label: GRADE
grade.description: Final academic mark on the project.

duration.label: DURATION
duration.description: Solo build over a full school year.

legacy.label: LEGACY
legacy.description: Technical report still used as school reference.

caseStudy.problem: Show users where it's risky to walk, based on what other users have flagged.
caseStudy.solution: React Native with Google Maps. Anyone can drop a pin on a dangerous spot. Other users vote, false positives get removed. Designed and built solo over a school year.
caseStudy.impact: 19/20 final grade. Five years later the school still points students at the report.

lucasmigueis-portfolio.description: Personal portfolio in Next.js 16. Bilingual, with a custom design system and a handful of interactive details.
lucasmigueis-portfolio.overview: Personal portfolio in Next.js 16, fully bilingual (<emphasis>EN + PT</emphasis>). Custom design tokens in Tailwind 4 and a few small interactive details.

locales.label: LOCALES
locales.description: EN · PT-PT, full content coverage including case studies.

lighthouse.label: LIGHTHOUSE
lighthouse.description: Perfect score across performance, a11y, and SEO.

tokens.label: TOKENS
tokens.description: Semantic color and typography tokens, zero arbitrary Tailwind values.

deps.label: DEPS
deps.description: Lean dependency footprint, every package justified.

caseStudy.problem: Most dev portfolios are either link dumps or template clones. I wanted something that shows I can ship technical work and design well, in two languages.
caseStudy.solution: Built from zero on Next.js 16 with Cache Components. Full EN + PT-PT through next-intl. Tailwind 4 with semantic tokens, no arbitrary values anywhere. Accessibility baked in. The hero has an animated code panel, the buttons show their own classes on hover, the nav highlights the section you're reading.
caseStudy.impact: A surface I can point people at, that also happens to exercise most of what's new in Next.js 16.

now.title: Now
now.subtitle: FOCUS
now.description: What I'm working on and thinking about right now.

working-on.body: Frontend lead on Portugal's new Government address change service (SAM) at Glintt Global.

building.body: This portfolio in Next.js 16 exploring Cache Components and the new motion library.

open-to.body: Talking to people about real product work. Freelance, full-time, or somewhere in between.

glintt-fs.description: Frontend lead for Portugal's new address change service (SAM). Next.js 15, accessibility, Kubernetes, 86% test coverage.

glintt-net.description: Backend on Chave Móvel Digital, Portugal's national auth, used by 8M+ citizens. Legacy .NET 4, REST/SOAP.

thinkfuture.description: Built an internal employee management app in React Native, end-to-end. Turned Figma prototypes from the UI/UX team into working screens.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.description: Open to freelance, full-time roles and side collaborations.
